The cutoff list was released category-wise as opening-closing cutoff ranks. The cutoff ranks also varied on the basis of gender, as some reservations were made for Female category students.

CG PET cutoff 2025 is finally out for students seeking admission to various undergraduate engineering courses in the state of Chhattisgarh. Chhattisgarh Institute of Technology, Kabirdham has also released the second round cutoff list for aspiring candidates, based on JEE Main scores and CG PET counselling. faqs.question: What is the eligibility to get admission in Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research for UG/PG courses?
faqs.answer:
Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:
Courses Eligibility
B.E. / B.Tech Candidate must have passed Diploma in relevant stream from a recognised Institute.
MBA/PGDM Candidate must have completed Bachelor degree of minimum 3 years duration with above mentioned marks from any recognised University/Institute. 45% marks in case of candidates belonging to reserved category.
MCA Candidate must have completed BCA/ Bachelor Degree in Computer Science Engineering or equivalent Degree with above mentioned marks from any recognised University/Institute. OR Candidate who have completed B.Sc./ B.Com./ B.A. with Mathematics at 10+2 Level or at Graduation Level can also apply. 45% marks in case of candidates belonging to reserved category.
After 10th Diploma Candidate passed 10th class/equivalent conducted by BSE, Odisha/equivalent are eligible to apply. The lower age limit is 14 years and there is no upper age limit.
UG Diploma Candidate passed 10th class/equivalent conducted by BSE, Odisha/equivalent are eligible to apply. The lower age limit is 14 years and there is no upper age limit.

faqs.question: Does Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has good and experienced faculty?
faqs.answer: Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has been rated 3.8 for faculty as per reviews by students. This represents that Institute has decent, qualified and experienced faculty members.

faqs.question: How is the infrastructure at Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research? What are the facilities available for students?

Institute has a well supported campus with facilities such as Boys Hostel, Cafeteria, Girls Hostel, Hospital / Medical Facilities, Hostel, Labs, Library, Shuttle Service, Sports Complex, Wi-Fi Campus. Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has also been rated 3.5 for Campuslife.

faqs.question: How is the placement facility at Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research?
faqs.answer: Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has been rated 1.8 on placements.

A:    Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has courses such as B.Tech, MBA/PGDM, MCA, After 1th Diploma, UG Diploma which are primarily in the stream of Engineering, Business & Management Studies, IT & Software. Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has specializations such as B.Tech specializations in Civil Engineering, Electronics Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, MBA/PGDM specializations in Sales & Marketing, After 10th Diploma specializations in Electrical Engineering, Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, UG Diploma specializations in Civil Engineering.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Civil Engineering - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Our college has good infrastructure, and it conducts robotics classes.

review_content.Placements: In our batch, only 5% to 10% of students were placed. Companies that visited our campus for recruitment were not that good. No one got an internship from our course. It did not matter a lot because no one applied for placements.
review_content.Infrastructure: The computer labs were in good condition. Wi-Fi was available throughout the campus. The library was in very good condition, with all types of books. Playgrounds for volleyball, badminton, and badminton were in good condition. Medical facilities were good.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Review of Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research.

review_content.Placements: Almost 25% of the students got placed in college. Around 30 to 40% of students got placed in companies like MRF, j.K Paper Mill, and Infosys. Roles offered are as junior engineers and advisers. A good placements are offered in Pune, Maharashtra, New Delhi, and Odisha. Vedant Pvt Ltd, HAL Ltd, etc., were the top recruiters of our course. It is a good college to study.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college has good labs to work, and we also have presentations in the classrooms. Libray is very huge and has a lot of books. The campus is also huge, and we have gardens and lawns. There are many other things on campus. Free Wi-Fi facilities are provided in our college. The office building is also nearby.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: I am dissatisfied with the college because it is not providing any placement opportunities.

review_content.Placements: The college doesn't provide any campus placements. Students got reduced because of fewer faculty. None of the companies visited our campus for placement in recent years. Students are doing other courses and taking other coaching for job opportunities.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college is well -facilitated Wi-Fi and computer labs. Mechanical labs and electrical labs are well-equipped. Classrooms and department blocks are well-designed and maintained. The hostel is also well-maintained with hygienic food and proper furniture. During the annual fest, sports, games and activities are conducted.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2017
reviews_list.review_title: Join this college only for a degree.

review_content.Placements: No placement is provided at all, not even a single company come to the college. Before 2012, companies used to come to the college. But nowadays, no company is coming here. Our senior batch has got good placement. They only promise about the placements, but they don't provide. Diploma and +2 courses are also provided on the same campus.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good. Wi-Fi is available everywhere. The classrooms are good. There is an A/C library with sufficient books. Food quality is good. The separate buildings are available for all the branches. The mechanical workshop is good. There is an on-campus hostel provided with average food. The college provides average transportation. We have no study tour, no fun.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Information Technology - Batch of 2014
reviews_list.review_title: GIACR college is best to complete your B.Tech. However, placements should not be expected.

review_content.Placements: We had a placement section for namesake. They gave us the required training for placements and getting jobs. However, we did not get a chance to get into any companies as placements were all fake and were the same for the past two years and remained the same for our juniors too.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college had the required infrastructure for a good B.Tech college. Excellent computers lab and communication lab is also there. We had Wi-Fi enabled campus. The mechanical workshop too was well-equipped. The campus had a hostel within our premises. We have a cafeteria which provides food that worth our money.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering - Batch of 2013
reviews_list.review_title: Dissatisfied with the college placements, and average regarding study, faculty, and other amenities.

review_content.Placements: During my time, the placements were not good. Even a lot of fake companies visited the campus. However, a few BPO companies visited, which are good. The average package offered was 1.8 lacs per annum. You have to try on your own and try to get the offer from the off-campus.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure on the campus is awesome. The college is spread over a big sprawling area. The food quality is good as compared to the other colleges in that area. The college has two big hostels for boys and two hostels for girls as well. There are the separate buildings for the CSE, ECE, and mechanical departments.

paragraphs: Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research admission2025 is both merit and entrance-based.Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Researchoffers 14 courses to the students across various specialisations. Courses are offered at UG, PG and After 10th levels.

At the UG level, Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research offers various courses, such asB.E. / B.Tech, UG Diploma, and many more. Similarly, at the PG level, students can pursue various courses, such asMBA/PGDM, MCA, etc.
The duration of UG courses is 3 years - 4 years. For PG programmes, the duration depends on the specialisations and typically is 2 years - 3 years.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility to get admission in Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research for UG/PG courses?
faqs.answer:
Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:CoursesEligibilityB.E. / B.TechCandidate must have passed Diploma in relevant stream from a recognised Institute.MBA/PGDMCandidate must have completed Bachelor degree of minimum 3 years duration with above mentioned marks from any recognised University/Institute.
45% marks in case of candidates belonging to reserved category.MCACandidate must have completed BCA/ Bachelor Degree in Computer Science Engineering or equivalent Degree with above mentioned marks from any recognised University/Institute.
OR
Candidate who have completed B.Sc./ B.Com./ B.A. with Mathematics at 10+2 Level or at Graduation Level can also apply.
45% marks in case of candidates belonging to reserved category.After 10th DiplomaCandidate passed 10th class/equivalent conducted by BSE, Odisha/equivalent are eligible to apply.
The lower age limit is 14 years and there is no upper age limit.UG DiplomaCandid

reviews.review_heading: Our college has good infrastructure, and it conducts robotics classes.
reviews.review_text: The computer labs were in good condition. Wi-Fi was available throughout the campus. The library was in very good condition, with all types of books. Playgrounds for volleyball, badminton, and badminton were in good condition. Medical facilities were good.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Gandhi Institute of Advanced Computer and Research.
reviews.review_text: Our college has good labs to work, and we also have presentations in the classrooms. Libray is very huge and has a lot of books. The campus is also huge, and we have gardens and lawns. There are many other things on campus. Free Wi-Fi facilities are provided in our college. The office building is also nearby.

reviews.review_heading: I am dissatisfied with the college because it is not providing any placement opportunities.
reviews.review_text: The college is well -facilitated Wi-Fi and computer labs. Mechanical labs and electrical labs are well-equipped. Classrooms and department blocks are well-designed and maintained. The hostel is also well-maintained with hygienic food and proper furniture. During the annual fest,  sports, games and activities are conducted.

reviews.review_heading: Join this college only for a degree.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is good. Wi-Fi is available everywhere. The classrooms are good. There is an A/C library with sufficient books. Food quality is good. The separate buildings are available for all the branches. The mechanical workshop is good. There is an on-campus hostel provided with average food. The college provides average transportation. We have no study tour, no fun.

reviews.review_heading: GIACR college is best to complete your B.Tech. However, placements should not be expected.
reviews.review_text: Our college had the required infrastructure for a good B.Tech college. Excellent computers lab and communication lab is also there. We had Wi-Fi enabled campus. The mechanical workshop too was well-equipped. The campus had a hostel within our premises. We have a cafeteria which provides food that worth our money.

reviews.review_heading: Dissatisfied with the college placements, and average regarding study, faculty, and other amenities.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure on the campus is awesome. The college is spread over a big sprawling area. The food quality is good as compared to the other colleges in that area. The college has two big hostels for boys and two hostels for girls as well. There are the separate buildings for the CSE, ECE, and mechanical departments.

They have their own hostels separately for boys and girls. They have their own guest house for parents. They have their own buses to carry students from hostel to college.

highlights.summary:
The TG ICET 2026 registration deadline has been extended for admission to the MBA and MCA courses. Students can now register till Mar 23, 2026, (without a late fee) for the exam scheduled for May 13 and May 14, 2026.

Below are a few more updates related to the Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology:

faqs.answer: A:    Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology has courses such as MBA/PGDM, M.E./M.Tech, B.Tech which are primarily in the stream of Business & Management Studies, Engineering. Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology has specializations such as M.E./M.Tech specializations in VLSI Design, Computer Science Engineering, Civil Engineering, Electrical Engineering, B.Tech specializations in Civil Engineering, Computer Science Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Mechanical Engineering.
faqs.question: Which courses are offered by Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology?

faqs.answer: A:    Courses offerred by Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology are approved by eminent Government bodies like AICTE. The combined seats available for courses offered at Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology goes up to 510.
faqs.question: How many seats are available for various courses offered at Dhruva Institute of Engineering and Technology?

reviews_list.review_title: I am dissatisfied with everything in this college.

review_content.Placements: Only half of the percentage of students get placements every time. It is the worst college. The salary offered is also low. Students score low percentage. Students get an internship on their own.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college does not provide Wi-Fi facility for students. The library is only for showing purpose. Classrooms are somewhat good and neat. Labs are not equipped with all equipment. Food served in the hostel is not good. Only outdoor games are available for the students. Medical facilities are available.
review_content.Faculty: Faculty members teaching quality is very low. Some teachers studied in this college only after completing their post-graduation, then they joined the same college for teaching. They don't plan a relevant curriculum. They just focus on teaching and forward the subjects for some lessons. They do not have knowledge.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Electronics and Communication Engineering - Batch of 2015
reviews_list.review_title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of infrastructure.

review_content.Placements: It is one of the waste colleges. No one got placed in my batch. All my friends did different courses in different institutions to get placed. And last but not the least it is no more operating now. College management gave their building to government institute for lease.
review_content.Infrastructure: There is nothing to describe about that school. It is a small square. No Wi-Fi in hostel not even in college. No adequate equipments in labs and no books library and they used to provide taste less food in hostel. In my 4 years I didn't heard about medical facilities.

reviews_list.batch_info: M.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2018
reviews_list.review_title: The college is good to score marks, and the campus has a peaceful environment.

review_content.Placements: Little placements are provided by the college, i.e., like BPO's, but they inform about the placements done by pool campus outside the college. Only little strength can be seen in college, and no one had interest in placements provided by the college. They came to complete their degree here, and the staff helps greatly to score good marks in the internals.
review_content.Infrastructure: Here, we can see everything in small college, and there is enough number of infrastructure I can say more than enough because of a low strength in college. Many colleges don't have enough infrastructure, but here we can see more classes and benches, than students. There is a basketball court to play, and there is enough ground to play cricket.

faqs.answer: A:    Courses offerred by Guru Nanak Institutions are approved by eminent Government bodies like AICTE. The combined seats available for courses offered at Guru Nanak Institutions goes up to 495.
faqs.question: How many seats are available for various courses offered at Guru Nanak Institutions?

faqs.answer:
A:    Guru Nanak Institutions has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityBBACandidate must have passed 10+2 or equivalent examination from a recognised school/board.B.PharmaCandidate must have minimum age 17 years or more on 31-12-2023.
Candidates who have qualified any other qualification approved by the Pharmacy Council of India equivalent to any of the above examinations are also eligible to apply.M.E./M.TechCandidates completed B.Tech./B.E. in relevant branch with at least 55% marks (50% for SC/ST candidates) from any recognised university are eligible to apply. Candidates completed 3 Year MCA course with 55% marks or 2 Year M.Sc. course with 55% marks from any recognised university are also eligible to apply.B.E. / B.TechCandidate must have passed 10+2 examination from recognised Board/University with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects along with one of the following subjects: Chemistry/Bio-Technology/Biology/Technical Vocational subject/Computer Science/Information Technology/Informatics Practices/Agriculture/Engineering Graphics/Business Studies. 
Candidate must have obtained at least 45% marks (40% in case of candidate belonging to reserved category) in the above subjects taken together.
OR 
Candidate must have passed Diploma (in Engineering and Technology) examination with at least 45% marks (40% in case of candidates belonging to reserved category).

review_summary.college_name: Guru Nanak Institutions Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Earlier it was good until 2016, but now it seems to be degrading. I don't recommend it to others.

content.Placements: Very few students, i.e., about 10% of the students from our CSE course get placed from our college. The highest salary package offered here is around 6 LPA. The lowest salary offered ranges from Rs. 12,000-15,000 per month. There is no such top recruiter or mass recruiter for our course. Students got internships, and some of them got them through our college, but most of them got through online platforms like Internshala and others. Majorly developers and software engineers roles are offered.
content.Infrastructure: Free Wi-Fi facility is available only for hostellers here. Labs are good, and classrooms are also good. There are good books in the library, but they are not updated. However, all students get books from the library, and they get books related to the course and other books also. The infrastructure of our college is average, and the campus is not huge but fine. Hostel rooms are quite huge, and the food served in the mess is fine. There is a menu for a week, which repeats. There is only one shop in the name of the canteen, and there is stationery, which is open during college time only. The hostel is very much far from the nearest market, i.e., about 3 km away. There is no transport facility, and even autos are not available. So, you have to walk 3 km or use your own vehicle. There is no medical support, but if any student has any issue, a quick transport facility is available to take them to the nearest hospital. Our college has one volleyball ground, a basketball court and a football field. There is a free gym for hostellers, which has average equipment, and the maintenance is not done on time.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Not fully satisfied because of the faculty, and the placements were much better in 2015.

content.Placements: B.Tech in CSE is an amazing course to learn good coding and to be successful in life. This college is affiliated to Kurukshetra University, and the placements are somehow average with 40% of the students getting placed with 2 LPA package in our course.
content.Infrastructure: The facilities and infrastructure provided to us are good. Facilities like good Wi-Fi, labs with a proper internet connection, A/C classrooms and a library with 3,500 books are available. The hostel mess and canteen are good. Sports kits are provided to us in the college.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: Guru Nanak Institutions is the best to get exposure.

content.Placements: GNI organise international tours to many countries every year. Also, GNI provides internships in different countries as I went to Russia for a 2-month internship and my experience was awesome. The overall placements of the institute are good. Last year, the placement record was really high.
content.Infrastructure: Every classroom is equipped with projectors, and there are 5 labs in every department. There are 3-4 seminar halls in the institute. The college cafeteria is big, and the library is okay. Sports department is not that good as there are just different courts for different games.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: The college provides knowledge and attractive lectures, sports, NCC camp, and international tours.

content.Placements: Placements of the college are very good. Recently, Policy Bazar visited our institute, and approximately 10-12 students got placed. There is a special placement cell in our institute. Our institute also provides NCC camp which provides to be a good career option.
content.Infrastructure: All rooms and buildings are maintained very nicely and are attractive. Also, they are very clean. There are labs where all the equipment is present. The labs are a good source to obtain knowledge. There is a cafeteria which is the best place to chill out with friends. There is also stationery in the institute. There is an open-air theatre available where all the events are organised.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: It provides more exposure. Students are average. They always work on discipline of institute.

content.Placements: According to me, the placements of our college are average. An average number of companies come to the college for placements. The placement cell is good, and the pattern of conducting placements is also good and well-planned. Every year, about 70% of the students got placed in mediocre companies.
content.Infrastructure: The labs are well-managed. The campus has its own Wi-Fi facility providing students free internet. Classrooms are good, and the library of B.Tech department is good as compared to the management department. Sports equipment are provided to students.

description: Guru Nanak Institutions cutoff 2025 is available on this page for admission to the BTech program. The cutoffs are available in the form of ranks for different categories of students belonging to the AI and HS quotas. Candidates interested in taking admission at Guru Nanak Institutions can check out the sections below to view the exam, course, and category-wise cutoff details. A few more updates are below:

The Guru Nanak Institutions cutoff 2024 was concluded for JEE Main with the release of the final cutoff list. The cutoff was released as the opening and closing ranks for admission to the BE/BTech, courses. During the Guru Nanak Institutions BE/BTech cutoff 2024, the last-round closing rank was 324238 for the General HS category students. Of all the BE/BTech specialisations, the one that faced fierce competition was B.Tech. in Civil Engineering.

The JEE Main cutoff 2024 was released for the General, OBC, ST, SC, EWS, and PWD categories in the HS quota. Considering the last round 2024 cutoff, B.Tech. in Civil Engineering was the most sought-after BE/BTech specialization with a closing rank of 324238 for the general HS quota. The closing cutoff for the same stood at 317568 in 2022.

Guru Nanak Institutions accepts JEE Main scores, followed by JoSAA and CSAB counselling rounds for admission to BTech and Integrated BTech courses. Candidates seeking admission to Guru Nanak Institutions can refer to the table below for tentative JEE Main schedule.

Guru Nanak Institutions JEE Main cutoff 2024 is now over for BTech, Integrated BTech, and BArch courses. For JEE Main, a total of five counselling rounds were conducted. Additionally, two CSAB counselling rounds were also conducted to fill the vacant seats. Candidates can refer to the table below to download the consolidated year-wise counselling rounds for Guru Nanak Institutions.
Candidates can find the closing ranks for the Guru Nanak Institutions released based on HSTES in the table below. The closing rank for the General HS category candidates was closed at 324238.
The most stringent cutoff was for admission to B.Tech. in Civil Engineering, with the last round closing rank of 324238 for the General HS quota. In 2022, the cutoff rank for the same was 317568.

college_info.college_name: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone

highlights.summary: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone is an institute in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh. At the UG and PG levels, the institute offers 6 courses across 2 specialisations in Full Time mode. The popular Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone programs include B.E. / B.Tech, MBA/PGDM, MCA, BCA and BBA. Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone courses are provided in several streams including Engineering, Business & Management Studies and IT & Software, etc. The fees at Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone ranges between INR 2,45,000 to 4,95,000 for the different courses. The institute has a well-laid infrastructure with multiple facilities.

placements.overview:
Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone has released the 2025 placements information. According to the same, over 1,200 recruiters visited the campus during placements 2025 and offered jobs in over 50 industrial domains. The highest package was recorded at INR 26 LPA. On the other hand, the average package during Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone placements 2025 stood at INR 4.6 LPA.

The recorded ROI was 63% higher during the 2025 recruitment cycle. The top recruiters during the 2025 placements included Coca-Cola, Zomato, Grow, Wipro, Paisabazar, Nestle, Amul, Paytm, SBI General, Axis Bank, Muthoot Finance, HDFC Life, among several others.

admission_process.title: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone Admission Process 2025

paragraphs:
Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone course admissions are done based on entrance and merit depending on the course. The university accepts applications in offline mode only. Interested students can get theHi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstoneapplication form from the university campus. The form has to be submitted along with specified documents.
Students can pursue UG and PG programmes in fields of Engineering, Business and Management Studies and IT and Software, and more. To know about the eligibility of Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone courses, students can visit the website.

Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone fee structure consists of caution deposit, exam fee, hostel fee, and more. The mode of payment for Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone fees is online. Interested candidates can check below for more details on Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone admission:

courses_data.name: B.E. / B.Tech Admissions 2026
courses_data.description: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone B.E. / B.Techis offered to candidates at the UG level. Candidates are required to participate in the entrance-based admission process to be considered for the final admission offer. Candidates are shortlisted for the B.E. / B.Tech programme based on their performance in the entrance exam. Candidates who qualify in the admission process are eligible to secure their seat in the 4 years programme.

courses_data.name: BCA Admissions 2026
courses_data.description: BCA at Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone is a 3 years UG course in the field of IT & Software. Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone accepts students based on Sunstone Screening Test, CBSE 12th and UP 12th. The final selection for admission to the Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone BCA course is based on the student's performance in the mentioned exams. See below the eligibility for Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone BCA:

courses_data.name: BBA Admissions 2026
courses_data.description: TheBBAatHi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstoneis a 3 years programme offered to the students in the field of Business & Management Studies. Admission to this course is offered based on a valid score in Sunstone Screening Test, CBSE 12th and UP 12th. To complete the Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone admission process for the 2025 batch, selected candidates must pay the applicable course fee ofINR 2,85,000.

courses_data.name: MBA/PGDM Admissions 2026
courses_data.description: Candidates interested in the fields related to Business & Management Studies can enroll in theHi-Tech Institute of Engineering and Technology Powered by Sunstone MBA/PGDMprogramme, which lasts for 2 years. Candidates are not required to appear for any entrance exams as the admission process is merit-based. The final shortlist for admission to the MBA/PGDM programme is determined by the candidates' academic scores.

placement_data.title: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone Placement Overview 2025
paragraphs: Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone has released the placement stats for the Class of 2025. According to the report, the highest and average package offered during Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone placements 2025 stood at INR 26 LPA and INR 4.6 LPA, respectively. More than 1,200 companies visited the campus in 2025 and provided job offers in over 50 domains . Some of the top recruiters of Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone include Nestle, Amul, Paytm, SBI General, Axis Bank, Muthoot Finance, HDFC Life, Coca-Cola, Zomato, Grow, Wipro, Paisabazar, etc.

Sunstone has launched a Placement Opportunity Program. The program offers students access to over 200 assured placement opportunities , or a refund of their first-year tuition fee if placements don’t meet the commitment. The eligibility criteria for the Placement Opportunity Program (POP) are as follows:

Hi-Tech Institute of Engineering & Technology Powered by Sunstone brings together the best of learning and industry practices to ensure that every student is transformed into a professional. The institute focuses on skill development through different modules that are designed by industry experts.

It trains the students for a variety of job profiles through more than 100 hours of job-focused boot camps . To ensure that the placement-centric sessions are completed successfully, the University's placement team also monitors the dynamic changes occurring globally.

highlights.summary:
The AP EAPCET 2026 registration last date has been extended to Mar 24, 2026, (without a late fee) for S.S.N. Engineering College admission to the BTech and BPharma courses. The BTech exams are scheduled for May 12 to May 15, and on May 18, 2026, followed by the BPharma exam on May 19 and May 20, 2026.

A few more updates are given below:

faqs.answer: A:    The seats availability for offered courses at S.S.N. Engineering College goes up to 1220. Courses offerred by S.S.N. Engineering College are approved by eminent Government bodies such as AICTE.
faqs.question: What is the current seat capacity for the courses at the S.S.N. Engineering College?

reviews_list.review_title: Absolutely satisfied with faculty.

review_content.Placements: The highest package offered in this college is Rs. 8 LPA, the average package is Rs. 3.8 LPA, and the lowest package is Rs. 2.3 LPA per annum. TCS, Wipro, and Infosys are recruiting companies. Around 20% of students got internships from Egipto.
review_content.Infrastructure: There are no Wi-Fi facilities, but the labs and classrooms are good. A library is also available. The facilities of the hostel are not good, and the food is okay. There are no medical facilities.
review_content.Faculty: Teachers are qualified to give extra information. The teaching quality is excellent. This course is curriculum relevant. It makes the students industry-ready. Semester exams are quite difficult in my college. The pass percentage is 90%.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Overall, my college is no good for upcoming students.

review_content.Placements: The highest package offered in my course is 8 Lacs, the average package is 3.8 Lacs and the lowest package is 2.3 Lacs per annum. TCS, Wipro, Infosys are recruiting Companies. Around 20 per cent of students got internships from Egipto. Software engineer profile is offered in my course.
review_content.Infrastructure: On my campus, there are no Wi-Fi facilities, labs and classrooms are good. A library is also available. The facilities of the hostel are not good and the food is okay but the canteen is not good. There are no medical facilities. There is no sports class but the ground is there.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2021
reviews_list.review_title: Overall, the college is average.

review_content.Placements: There are no placements. The management did not provide placements for students. They focus only on money. They collect fees and assure placements. There are no proper guidelines for teaching. The teachers did not take the classes properly.
review_content.Infrastructure: There is no hostel in this college. There is no proper library to study. There are no bus facilities for students. We have to commute by auto or RTC buses. There is no canteen.
review_content.Faculty: The teaching quality is bad. There is a drainage system beside the college, and the staff and students suffer a lot from the smell. The college looks huge. There are no proper fans and lights to study at night. It is a huge problem in the rainy season. I suggest not to join this college.

review_summary.college_name: S.S.N. Engineering College Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Electronics Engineering - Batch of 2017
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely satisfied with faculty.

content.Placements: The highest package offered in this college is Rs. 8 LPA, the average package is Rs. 3.8 LPA, and the lowest package is Rs. 2.3 LPA per annum. TCS, Wipro, and Infosys are recruiting companies. Around 20% of students got internships from Egipto.
content.Infrastructure: There are no Wi-Fi facilities, but the labs and classrooms are good. A library is also available. The facilities of the hostel are not good, and the food is okay. There are no medical facilities.
content.Faculty: Teachers are qualified to give extra information. The teaching quality is excellent. This course is curriculum relevant. It makes the students industry-ready. Semester exams are quite difficult in my college. The pass percentage is 90%.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: Overall, my college is no good for upcoming students.

content.Placements: The highest package offered in my course is 8 Lacs, the average package is 3.8 Lacs and the lowest package is 2.3 Lacs per annum. TCS, Wipro, Infosys are recruiting Companies. Around 20 per cent of students got internships from Egipto. Software engineer profile is offered in my course.
content.Infrastructure: On my campus, there are no Wi-Fi facilities, labs and classrooms are good. A library is also available. The facilities of the hostel are not good and the food is okay but the canteen is not good. There are no medical facilities. There is no sports class but the ground is there.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: Overall, the college is average.

content.Placements: There are no placements. The management did not provide placements for students. They focus only on money. They collect fees and assure placements. There are no proper guidelines for teaching. The teachers did not take the classes properly.
content.Infrastructure: There is no hostel in this college. There is no proper library to study. There are no bus facilities for students. We have to commute by auto or RTC buses. There is no canteen.
content.Faculty: The teaching quality is bad. There is a drainage system beside the college, and the staff and students suffer a lot from the smell. The college looks huge. There are no proper fans and lights to study at night. It is a huge problem in the rainy season. I suggest not to join this college.

reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is not good. Classrooms have a very dull atmosphere. Labs are not well-equipped. The library is very old and not comfortable for students. reviews.review_heading: Awesome college to learn and enjoy.
reviews.review_text: There will be all the Aeronautical Labs with separate classes with a good environment. This college is near mountains with a superb natural view. All the facilities are available for an engineering student, and all the kind of sports including the swimming pool is available. There is also a separate hostel available year-wise and gender-wise.

Nice staff and good atmosphere. Located near Vandavasi, Thiruvannamalai District. faqs.answer: A:    After you have completed your BTech Food Technology course at a decent college in India, you can get some amazing earning opportunities in the business world. The average starting salary for fresh graduates like you is usually around INR 4 LPA. As you move forward in the work sector and gain some years of experience, you will start to earn more as recruiters will be ready to offer you a higher salary package because of your technical expertise. reviews_list.review_title: Our faculty is so caring and knowledgeable. They always try to provide best knowledge to us.

review_content.Placements: About 40% of students got placed in the campus interviews till now. The highest salary package offered was 8 LPA, the lowest salary package offered was 3.5 LPA, and the average salary package offered was 4 LPA - 5 LPA. Top recruiting companies were TCS, Infosys, Capgemini, IBM, Accenture, Ugam Solutions, Hexaware, and many more. About 20% of students got internships in small companies. The top roles offered in our course were data analyst and software engineer.
review_content.Infrastructure: We had Wi-Fi with good internet speed. We had 6 labs and each lab had Wi-Fi, a printer, and a scanner. There was a server room that handled all machines present in the six labs. Our college had a library. It also had a projector facility for presentations, workshops, and seminars.

faqs.answer: Courses offered by Siddartha Educational Academy Group of Institutions are M.E./M.Tech, B.Tech, MBA/PGDM. These courses are in the stream of Engineering, Business & Management Studies. Siddartha Educational Academy Group of Institutions offers specializations such as M.E./M.Tech specializations in Computer Science Engineering, Structural Engineering, B.Tech specializations in Civil Engineering, Computer Science Engineering, Electronics & Communication Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning.
faqs.question: Q: Which courses are available at Siddartha Educational Academy Group of Institutions for students?

faqs.answer: Siddartha Educational Academy Group of Institutions has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityM.E./M.TechCandidate must have passsed B.E./B.Tech. in relevant stream and obtained at least 50% marks (45% in case of reserved category candidates) from an AICTE/UGC approved institution recognised by the Government of Andhra Pradesh as equivalent thereto. Final year appearing students may also apply on provisional basis.B.E. / B.TechCandidates who have passed Diploma in Engineering or any other examination recognised as equivalent there to, from a recognised Institute/ University, Andhra Pradesh/ Telangana are also eligible. Candidates who have appeared in Diploma in Engineering and who are awaiting their results can also apply on provisional basis. Candidates should belong to the state of Andhra Pradesh/ Telangana. Candidate should have completed 16 years of age as of 31st December of the year of admission (2023). There is no upper age limit.MBA/PGDMCandidate must have completed Bachelor degree of 3/4 years duration with above mentioned marks from any recognised University/Institute. 45% marks for BC categories. Candidate who are appearing for the final year degree examination are also be eligible. Degree obtained by Distance Mode Program should have recognition by Joint Committee of UGC, AICTE and DEC/DEB. Candidate who passed all the subjects of the three years of any Degree course in one sitting is not eligible for admission.

college_info.intro:
Sushant University Powered by Sunstone offers a wide range of programmes in the field of Engineering & Technology. The college offers programmes to students in full-time mode and is affiliated with the GGSIPU. SU Gurgaon's flagship programmes include India's most experiential BTech programme.
The college offers a diverse range of programmes across several specialisations such as Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning, Data Science, Cyber Security, etc. Suntone powered Sushant University offers BTech for a total course fee worth INR 10.83 Lakh for the duration of 4 years in full-time mode. Admission to this course is based on merit. Therefore, candidates must secure a minimum aggregate of 50% in Class 12 with PCM. Candidates can check the Sushant University Powered by Sunstone popular programmes in the table given below:

placements.overview:
The institute concluded the 2024 placement session with over 5,000 dream jobs delivered. The highest package offered during Sushant University Powered by Sunstone placements in 2024 stood at INR 26 LPA. Additionally, over 1,200 companies visited the campus and offered jobs in more than 50 domains.

Sunstone’s Placement Opportunity Program (POP) offers students access to 200+ assured placement opportunities, or a refund of their first-year tuition fee if placements don’t meet the commitment. To be eligible for POP, students must maintain a minimum average attendance of 70% across all credit and non-credit classes in every semester. They are also required to clear all examinations with a minimum score of 80% or equivalent CGPA, and must not have any backlogs. To be eligible for POP, students must maintain a minimum average attendance of 70% across all credit and non-credit classes in every semester. They are also required to clear all examinations with a minimum score of 80% or equivalent CGPA, and must not have any backlogs.

review_summary.college_name: Christian College of Engineering and Technology (Bhilai) Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.E. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely dissatisfied due to the placements offered to 2024 passout batch.

content.Placements: All students in computer science engineering branch get placed every year. The lowest package offered here is 2.5 LPA. The average package lies between 3 LPA to 3.5 LPA. Top recruiting companies include Byjus and Fintech Companies. I don't know the exact number of students who get internships from our course through our college.
content.Infrastructure: There are many labs for computer science and engineering department. In classrooms, we have Wi-Fi connectivity. There is one central library and one common library. Each department has its own separate libraries which include the specific branch-wise books in them.

individual_reviews.course: B.E. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2021
individual_reviews.title: I am satisfied with this college.

content.Placements: Nearly 90% of students got selected by various core companies. The top recruiting companies were TCS, Wipro, Indian Navy, and Tech Mahindra. If somehow top companies did not allow students on campus placements, then this college refers them to walking drive. Around 50-60% of students were placed.
content.Infrastructure: I was a day scholar, so I did not know about hostel facilities. But I have heard from my friends that hostel facilities were normal. The library and computer laboratories were good.
content.Faculty: Faculty members were very good. They were friendly, and students can ask anything to clear their doubts. They were knowledgeable in their particular domain.

individual_reviews.title: It was not a good college for placements but the infrastructure was good.

content.Placements: Our college provided no placements. Only a few students got a chance to get placed. I was lucky to get placed. But eventually, I rejected the offer. The placement officer was not supportive at all. This college was not up to the mark when compared to other colleges in the city
content.Infrastructure: Facilities provided were great as compared to other engineering colleges in the city. There were well-developed labs. The playground was available for students, and the food served in the hostel mess was good. Sports facilities were available. The entire campus was surrounded by greenery. It also included all modern facilities like elevators and Wi-Fi. All the labs and library were huge. It was one of the best colleges.

The cutoff list was released category-wise as opening-closing cutoff ranks. The cutoff ranks also varied on the basis of gender, as some reservations were made for Female category students.

CG PET cutoff 2025 is finally out for students seeking admission to various undergraduate engineering courses in the state of Chhattisgarh. Christian College of Engineering and Technology (Bhilai) has also released the second round cutoff list for aspiring candidates, based on JEE Main scores and CG PET counselling. The minimum cutoff for admission to any branch was 1486, for candidates belonging to the General HS category. The maximum required cutoff rank is 3225 during Round 2 admission.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: The Techno School (TTS, Bhubaneswar) offers a wide range of programmes to students at the UG and PG levels. The college is mainly concerned with streams such as Engineering, Business & Management Studies and IT & Software. At the UG level, the college offers courses such as B.E. / B.Tech. At the PG level, the college offers courses such as M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM and MCA. The Techno School (TTS, Bhubaneswar) courses are offered to students in Full Time mode. Further, the fees structure for The Techno School (TTS, Bhubaneswar) courses stands around INR 79,500 to 2,00,000.

These programs are offerred in Full Time mode. review_content.General: No one got placement from our college since our college management had no interest in placing anyone. At the time of admission, they promised and said that, in this college, 100% placements are provided and that also in MNCs. My life got spoiled after joining here.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college has only one building which has everything inside it. There is no Wi-Fi facility for the students. The labs are not having all the equipment. The classrooms are the okay type. The library is good in look but not having all kinds of books. There is no medical facility available, and they don't even provide for any emergency purpose. Sports and game facilities are not there.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2014
reviews_list.review_title: Review

review_content.Other: Except infrastructure, nothing is good in this college. The campus placement is totally zero. There is a shortage of faculty members in each department. One teacher handles more than three subjects. The faculty talks in the regional language. Many times, they taught in Oriya language only, which is very difficult for other states’ students. The training and placement cell is just like a museum. Please think many times before joining this college. When I joined it in 2010, the rank of the college was #8, and when I completed my course in 2014, the rank of college was # 64. Rank and performance of the college is continuously declining every year. Academics are the worst. The fee is too high and the labs have less equipment. You have to depend on self-study only. The location of the college is nice, as it connects to the city. If you only want to get a degree, please go through it, otherwise don't.

review_summary.college_name: The Techno School (TTS, Bhubaneswar) Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Civil Engineering - Batch of 2015
individual_reviews.title: The placements in our college were good earlier.

content.Placements: The placements in our college are average as not many companies have visited the campus. Even though only a few students got placed, the salary packages offered by the companies which visited the campus were good. It's a good place to study, and it can be much better. Many courses like B.Tech, MBA, and diploma are offered.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of the college is great, and the college hostels are also nice. There is Wi-Fi facility available on the campus. CCTV cameras are installed in every classroom. A two storeyed-library with a lot of books is available. The labs are big and have a lot of free space. The campus is big enough and is neatly maintained. The floors are also maintained tidy.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2017
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely dissatisfied with the management.

content.Placements: No one got placement from our college since our college management had no interest in placing anyone. At the time of admission, they promised and said that, in this college, 100% placements are provided and that also in MNCs. My life got spoiled after joining here.
content.Infrastructure: The college has only one building which has everything inside it. There is no Wi-Fi facility for the students. The labs are not having all the equipment. The classrooms are the okay type. The library is good in look but not having all kinds of books. There is no medical facility available, and they don't even provide for any emergency purpose. Sports and game facilities are not there.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2014

content.Other: Except infrastructure, nothing is good in this college. The campus placement is totally zero. There is a shortage of faculty members in each department. One teacher handles more than three subjects. The faculty talks in the regional language. Many times, they taught in Oriya language only, which is very difficult for other states’ students. The training and placement cell is just like a museum. Please think many times before joining this college. When I joined it in 2010, the rank of the college was #8, and when I completed my course in 2014, the rank of college was # 64. Rank and performance of the college is continuously declining every year. Academics are the worst. The fee is too high and the labs have less equipment. You have to depend on self-study only. The location of the college is nice, as it connects to the city. If you only want to get a degree, please go through it, otherwise don't.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2015

content.Other: I had a worst experience in these 4 year of engineering. I could not get the proper knowledge and guidance, which I expected from the institution. The institution had very less teaching staff. Practical were also not done properly. Therefore, I strongly say no, to join this institute.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2011

content.Other: An average College, situated at the right place. Neither too far from city nor too close. Faculty wise- College recruits new Faculty members. Many of them joining college have passed out in recent times, out of job for long time looking for temporary faculty opportunity. Their concepts are not clear and expecting too much from them is a mistake from the student's side. College never takes it seriously. Few of the Faculty Members were good, though also had good level of experience in teaching but they never stayed with the college after 2013 and join other colleges for higher pay packages. Placement wise- College promises 80% placement which is simply fake. The placement is hardly 8%. Off-campuses are rare and On-Campuses are even rarer. Those who land themselves up with a job, if it involves college by any means, be it like if college sending you to off-campus or college bringing on-campus and you are getting a job via it, then you will have to pay back college a big amount later. When you pass out and go back to collect your certificates, college will ask you to pay 25-30K (rate of 2012-13) or else they will hold all your certificates & mark sheets. Say, privatized corruption. Infrastructure wise- College is average here. Located at a nice place with nice structure and has all lab requirements although it lacks faculty with experience to teach or explain you anything inside the labs. Most of them are clueless themselves. So don't expect too much. Research helps. This is just average because you can get your job done and forget it the next day. Good thing has always been the location, the friendly Faculty. Bad thing is less experienced faculty, poor placements, almost like playing with career of students. Canteen area is OK. You can get some relief there, if you're in First year. College fest are regularly organized and are also good. Gives enough opportunity to students to explore different areas which includes gaming, robotics, dance, singing etc. I studied Computer Science and passed out in 2011. When I took admission here in 2007, I had literally no option or colleges in hand and had to continue education without gap. That was a big mistake on my side. In the hope of saving a year, I lost many later. I don't recommend this college to any fresh school pass out candidate. Hefty fees will be charged from you and in return all you will get is nothing. College took around 8000 INR from each students for pre-placement training that was to be given in 4th year - 8th semester, but sadly they never gave any. Only 5 classes of GD/PI and 5 classes of Coding/C/C++/Aptitude was enough for college. When complained they kept it on hold and never cared. So basically, whatever we students gained was all because of our own strength and confidence. On Campus- We were one of the first batches to sit in On-campus, compared to any other college. It took place in 6th semester itself. When we were still in 3rd year, Company was ECS and it hired few, but those few never got any appointment letter and by the time we reached 8th semester, company had cancelled the appointments and everything. In 8th semester, we sat for Tech Mahindra which was the only good and branded Campus Placement Company, I would say. A handful few made it to Tech Mahindra. Other Campus Placements were by small companies. Most of them hired, but ended up cancelling appointment later, so not to be taken seriously. Off campus- 2 companies were there; L&T and Syntel. In L&T, very few made it, but had to pay a hefty money to college to get their certificates later. Same goes for Syntel. Study wise you'll not find passionate, research oriented students. Most of the students just want to get degrees and jump into the rat race which is one of the sad part of the education system itself. Even when you want to get exposure to research, your faculty will not at all help. College provided us Hostel services during 1st year only (For Boys) and for girls, it provides hostel services till 4th year. Hostel was OK. Fees was 28K, back in 2007 including food that included breakfast + lunch + dinner. Food was OK. Came straight from canteen and on time. Hostel was around 1.5 Kms away from college. It was a nice experience to live with many other students there and learn a lot of things and make friends. Although college never provided any strict timing or rules and whole hostel was like open ground to do anything. College should have maintained some good and strict rules by hiring a good warden. They took caution money for hostel but never returned the same after we left the hostel. According to me, this is not a nice place or Institute to study. I would not recommend students to invest time and money here. In case you couldn't land up a job for yourself, you'll be wasting a lot of time (not sure how many) after college. That's why I wrote - In the hope of saving 1 year, most of us lost many later. Many of my friends are still looking for a job and this is 2016. So invest good amount and time to studies and get into a much nicer college.

reviews_list.review_title: It's the worst college I have ever seen.

review_content.Placements: It's a useless college as there no placement opportunities. Joining the college on the basis of placements will be the worst decision. There's nothing much positive. Don't opt this the college for placements. Opt for the other colleges rather than this. None of the companies visited the college for placements.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college provides the worst infrastructure. Even the classrooms are not good. Classrooms are in bad condition. The campus doesn't look good. There are no boundaries at some of the area in the college. Overall the infrastructure is not good.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2015
reviews_list.review_title: If you are good at studying and belong to a very poor family, you can study at a very low price.

review_content.Placements: There are no placements in this college. When I graduated, no placements are available in this college. 1 single company came for an interview and selected 6 students out of 80. Even those 6 students also did not get a job or joining letter. I don't know the present situation and the development of the college.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is not good. Sports tho bhull hi jaooo. Medical facilities are average as we have a first-aid kit only, which is found in everyone's car. Classrooms are similar to the government school classrooms. The food (canteen) has no taste on food. Only hostel students eat there. The hostel is too far away from the college.

faqs.answer: Rattan Institute of Technology and Management (RITM, Haryana) has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityBBACandidate must have passed in 10+2 Examination or equivalent in any discipilne from any recognised Board.B.E. / B.TechCandidate must have passed Diploma in the relevant field of Engineering with minimum 60% from a recognised university.MBA/PGDMCandidate must have passed Post Graduate Degree in any discipline with not less than 50% marks ( 47.5 % is for SC/ST of Haryana candidates ) in aggregate or any examination recognised by M.D. University, Rohtak as equivalent thereto.UG DiplomaCandidate must have passed 10th Std/ SSC Examination from a recognised board.After 10th DiplomaCandidate must have passed 10th Std/ SSC Examination from a recognised board.M.E./M.TechCandidates completed B.E./B.Tech. or equivalent degree in Mechanical Engineering/Civil Engineering/Production Engineering/Thermal Power Engineering/ Automobile Engineering. / Robotics Engineering / CAD / Mechatronics / Aeronautical Engineering / Industrial Engineering with 50% Marks (45% marks for SC/ST candidates of Haryana only) are eligible to apply. Candidate must have also qualified GATE.

review_summary.college_name: Rattan Institute of Technology and Management (RITM, Haryana) Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: I would recommend joining this college to fulfill your future dreams.

content.Placements: Students must score 89% to get placed in companies. The highest salary package offered is 10 LPA, and the lowest salary package offered is 2.5 LPA. Top recruiting companies are Mitsuba and Victora Industries. Almost 80% of students got internships. Roles offered are production manager, quality manager, production engineer, and quality check engineer.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure of our college is very good. Our college provides good facilities like Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, a hostel, etc. The food served in the canteen is good. Medical facilities are available in our college.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Business Administration - Batch of 2015
individual_reviews.title: Our college is the best and I am satisfied with it.

content.Placements: Our college offers courses like BBA, MBA, B. Tech, M.Tech, and diploma. All Trade and ITI courses are also organised. The total number of students in the BBA course is 62. All the students are the best and hardworking. The fee charged in our course is Rs. 50,000 per annum and our college provide the best placements for every student. Our provides the best company interface. All the staff members put their best efforts to get the best results in every semester.
content.Infrastructure: Our college provided the best hostel. All the classrooms were fully air-conditioned with projectors installed. seating was also the best. Labs were available and we had the largest library in our college. There was a huge playground, plants, etc. All the classrooms were Wi-Fi enabled.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Civil Engineering - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: It's the worst college I have ever seen.

content.Placements: It's a useless college as there no placement opportunities. Joining the college on the basis of placements will be the worst decision. There's nothing much positive. Don't opt this the college for placements. Opt for the other colleges rather than this. None of the companies visited the college for placements.
content.Infrastructure: The college provides the worst infrastructure. Even the classrooms are not good. Classrooms are in bad condition. The campus doesn't look good. There are no boundaries at some of the area in the college. Overall the infrastructure is not good.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Mechanical Engineering - Batch of 2015
individual_reviews.title: If you are good at studying and belong to a very poor family, you can study at a very low price.

content.Placements: There are no placements in this college. When I graduated, no placements are available in this college. 1 single company came for an interview and selected 6 students out of 80. Even those 6 students also did not get a job or joining letter. I don't know the present situation and the development of the college.
content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is not good. Sports tho bhull hi jaooo. Medical facilities are average as we have a first-aid kit only, which is found in everyone's car. Classrooms are similar to the government school classrooms. The food (canteen) has no taste on food. Only hostel students eat there. The hostel is too far away from the college.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: The RITM is good because of faculty, facilities and lifetime support.

content.Placements: I have placed at Codeslipi infotech Pvt. Ltd. I thank my college for giving this opportunity. The best is that I got a placement at the college campus. There are a lot of options for students career. I placed in 3 top companies, but I selected Codes Lipi Infotech as PHP Developer.
content.Infrastructure: If I talk about the infrastructure, there are better buildings and playgrounds. The classrooms are very well-maintained and cleaned. I completed B.Tech, CSE, the computer science labs and classrooms are very well-maintained and always be in working support.

B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering turned out to be the most competitive branch for admission. In the first round, the cutoff rank for the same branch was 992869.
Considering the JEE Main last round cutoff 2025 for the General HS category students, the top most competitive course for admission was B.E. / B.Tech, with a cutoff rank closing at 599348.
For more categories, rounds and quotas, candidates must use the provided filters.
Some of the important dates related to JEE Main 2026 for admission are provided below. Candidates can keep track of the upcoming dates to be eligible for admission.

hostel_campus.college_name: Rattan Institute of Technology and Management (RITM

reviews.review_heading: I would recommend joining this college to fulfill your future dreams.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is very good. Our college provides good facilities like Wi-Fi, labs, classrooms, a hostel, etc. The food served in the canteen is good. Medical facilities are available in our college.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is the best and I am satisfied with it.
reviews.review_text: Our college provided the best hostel. All the classrooms were fully air-conditioned with projectors installed. seating was also the best. Labs were available and we had the largest library in our college. There was a huge playground, plants, etc. All the classrooms were Wi-Fi enabled.

reviews.review_heading: It's the worst college I have ever seen.
reviews.review_text: The college provides the worst infrastructure. Even the classrooms are not good. Classrooms are in bad condition. The campus doesn't look good. There are no boundaries at some of the area in the college. Overall the infrastructure is not good.

reviews.review_heading: If you are good at studying and belong to a very poor family,  you can study at a very low price.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is not good. Sports tho bhull hi jaooo. Medical facilities are average as we have a first-aid kit only, which is found in everyone's car. Classrooms are similar to the government school classrooms. The food (canteen) has no taste on food. Only hostel students eat there. The hostel is too far away from the college.

reviews.review_heading: The RITM is good because of faculty, facilities and lifetime support.
reviews.review_text: If I talk about the infrastructure, there are better buildings and playgrounds. The classrooms are very well-maintained and cleaned. I completed B.Tech, CSE, the computer science labs and classrooms are very well-maintained and always be in working support.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is a great college.
reviews.review_text: It has 5 buildings, for computer science engineering, civil, mechanical engineering, electrical engineering and the last one is MBA department. It has wheel equipped labs, classrooms, library and a high-speed Wi-Fi also.

reviews.review_heading: It is an average college.
reviews.review_text: Classrooms are totally old, and it is not air-conditioned. There are no good boards, and they use chalk systems with full dust in classrooms, and it does not look better. The building is old. Only a 10% Wi-Fi facility is available on the campus. There is no network.

reviews.review_heading: Really good infrastructure, facilities and well educated staff.
reviews.review_text:
1. Civil Engineering labs are available I. e Surveying lab with instruments, Soil Mechanics Lab, Building material testing Lab etc. All required instruments and lab are available. 
2. I did not use hostel services. Canteen Service good.
A large size open playground is available. 
Bus facility is available.

college_info.intro: The flagship programmes offered at St. Joseph University Dimapur include BBA and MBA. For BBA admissions, students must have passed Class 12 with at least 40% aggregate. On the other hand, for MBA admissions, students must have any undergraduate degree from a recognised university with at least 40% aggregate. Students can check the popular programmes offered at St. Joseph University Dimapur in the table provided below:

faqs.answer: A:    Candidates do not require passing CAT for admission to MBA programme at St. Joseph University Dimapur. Aspirants only need to meet the eligibility requirements to get admission. Candidates who have passed a UG degree from a recognised university with at least 40% aggregate can apply for MBA.
faqs.question: Is CAT required at St. Joseph University Dimapur?

faqs.answer: A:    Candidates who have passed BCA/ BSc Computer Science/ BSc Information Technology/ BSc Mathematics, BSc Physics, BSc Chemisry with a minimum of 40% aggregate can apply for the MCA programme at St. Joseph University Dimapur. Aspirants must have relevant documents proving their eligibility for verification at the time of admission.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility for MCA at St. Joseph University?

faqs.answer: A:    The total tuition fee for BA (Hons) at St. Joseph University Dimapur is INR 1.2 lakh. Aspirants who are shortlisted for admission have to confirm their seat by paying the admission and tuition fee. Candidates have to pay the fee via the mode specified by the university.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Mathematics - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Life in St. Joseph University.

review_content.Placements: Minimum 70% of students were placed in our course. The highest package offered in our course is Rs. 80K to 1 Lakh, the lowest package offered is Rs. 30K to Rs. 50K, and the average package is Rs. 50K to Rs. 80K. Till now, we don't have top recruiting companies for our course.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure at St. Joseph University is satisfactory and well-maintained. All kinds of necessary facilities are available for the students. The quality of facilities in our hostel is acceptable. The quality of food in the mess and canteen is good and hygienic. All kinds of medical, sports, and games facilities are available at our university.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.Tech. in Civil Engineering - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: Not satisfied with teachers of St. Joseph University.

review_content.Placements: Only 10% of the students got placed. The highest package offered was INR 2.4 LPA, while the lowest was INR 1.4 LPA, and the average package was around INR 1.8 LPA. About 50% of the students secured internships, and the internship experience is considered quite good.
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities in the college are adequate and well-maintained for my department. The labs are in good condition, classrooms are well-equipped, and the library has a vast collection of books. Wi-Fi is available throughout the campus. The quality of food in the canteen is decent, and the sports facilities are average.

fee_structure.overview: St. Joseph University offers students courses in different areas. Candidates can enroll in UG and PG programmes based on their interests and career goals. Admissions are offered based on merit or entrance exam performance. Check below the St. Joseph University courses along with their tuition fees and eligibility criteria:

faqs.answer: St. Joseph University Dimapur offers admission to candidates based on their merit. Aspirants meeting the eligibility criteria for the applied course can get admission provided he or she is able to prove their eligibility at the time of verification. Selected candidates have to confirm their seat by paying the course fee.
faqs.question: Q: Can I get direct admission at St. Joseph University Dimapur?

faqs.answer: St. Joseph University Dimapur offers admission to candidates based on their merit to UG and PG courses. Hence, aspirants do not require to pass an entrance exam for these courses. However, aspirants do need to pass a preliminary interview via phone by the online admission desk for final selection. Shortlisted aspirants after completing all the rounds need to confirm their seat by paying the course fee.
faqs.question: Q: Can I get admission at St. Joseph University Dimapur without entrance exam?

faqs.answer: Candidates can apply at St. Joseph University Dimapur by paying an application fee of INR 400. Aspirants have to pay the fee via any of the online payment modes. Candidates are advised to keep the fee receipt with them for any future reference.
faqs.question: Q: How much is the application fee at St. Joseph University Dimapur?
